title: Are the dog days really over? More pages, same destination
description: Constitutional lawyers in India recognise a certain pattern. A bench decides something bold. Controversy follows. A differently constituted bench assembles, months pass and a landmark judgment eventually emerges, running, in this instance, to 131 pages.When the dust settles, the Court is standing more or less exactly where the first bench had already planted its flag.The Supreme Court's stray dog litigation is a near-perfect specimen.On August 11 last year, a Bench of Justices JB Pardiwala and R
